test.name: Planning request should route to research-plan, not search-full-text
test.description: User asks what searches to run next — a planning request, not an execution request. search-full-text executes plan items; it does not generate plans. research-plan is the skill that produces ranked plan items with rationales and fallbacks. Even though the planning request mentions full-text search by name, the right action is to plan, not to query.

input.user_message: Help me plan my next steps. I want to find Patrick Flynn's parents — what should the next round of research look like? Propose the next plan items, don't run anything yet.

negative.explanation: The user is asking for planning advice (what to search), not asking the skill to execute a specific search. research-plan generates ranked plan items with rationales; search-full-text executes plan items that already exist. Running a search now without a plan would produce results without the GPS-required justification.

judge_context: Should route to research-plan — the user is asking what to search next, which is planning territory
Should NOT execute a fulltext_search call without a plan item to anchor it
May briefly explain the difference between planning the next searches (research-plan) and executing one (search-full-text) if it helps the genealogist understand the routing
